paper_title: Pollen, biomes, forest successions and climate at Lake Barombi Mbo (Cameroon) during the last ca. 33 000 cal yr BP - a numerical approach

methods.description: This method is used to reconstruct climate parameters from fossil assemblages along sedimentary sequences or for key periods. It involves calculating a chord distance to measure dissimilarity between fossil and modern spectra, identifying best modern analogues, and averaging climatic parameters with a weighting inverse to the distance.

steps.description: Replace values of the taxa percentages with values of the PFTs scores.
steps.searchable_summary: convert taxa percentages to PFT scores

steps.description: Calculate a score for each PFT for both modern and fossil spectra.
Calculate chord distance to measure dissimilarity between each fossil spectrum and all modern ones.
steps.searchable_summary: compute chord distance between fossil and modern spectra

steps.description: Identify modern spectra with the smallest distance as the best modern analogues for each fossil pollen spectrum.
steps.searchable_summary: find best modern analogues based on smallest chord distance

steps.description: Average climatic parameters associated with best analogues with a weighting inverse to the distance.
steps.searchable_summary: average climatic parameters with inverse distance weighting

methods.description: This method uses artificial neural networks to calibrate non-linear relationships between plant functional types (PFTs) and climatic variables. It is considered more robust than taxon-climate calibration and is used to infer climatic variables from fossil PFT scores.

steps.description: Transfer pollen counts into PFT scores.
steps.searchable_summary: convert pollen counts to PFT scores

steps.description: Calibrate PFT scores derived from modern pollen data in terms of climatic parameters using an artificial neural network.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate PFT scores with neural network

steps.description: Apply neural network coefficients to fossil PFT scores to infer climatic variables.
steps.searchable_summary: apply neural network to infer climatic variables

methods.description: This method involves extracting fossil data from sedimentary sequences, calibrating radiocarbon dates into calendar ages, and establishing a polynomial-age model to cover the sedimentary sequence.

steps.description: Extract fossil data from the sedimentary sequence BM-6.
steps.searchable_summary: extract fossil data from sedimentary sequence

steps.description: Calibrate radiocarbon dates into calendar ages using CALIB 5.0.1 software.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate radiocarbon dates to calendar ages

steps.description: Establish a second-order polynomial-age model along the sequence.
